ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact 'Fish Tikka' entry in FoodOn; 'grilled fish' (FOODON_00002999) is the closest parent category for this tandoor-grilled marinated fish preparation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Fish Tikka; A00LC (Fish and fish products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for Fish Tikka as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Fish Tikka consists of 3 to 4 marinated and grilled fish pieces, often served as an appetizer or a light main course.

nutrient_source.title: Fish, cod, Atlantic, cooked, dry heat
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data is adapted from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 172093) for plain grilled cod, with adjustments made to energy, fat, carbohydrates, sugar, dietary fibre, and sodium to account for the typical yogurt-based marinade and spices used in Fish Tikka preparation. Micronutrients not reported in this adapted context are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Database (various fish and dairy products)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is estimated by analogy. Fish is a protein food with effectively zero GI. The small amount of carbohydrates from the yogurt marinade and spices results in a very low overall glycemic impact. Glycemic index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value) as per convention for effectively zero-GI foods. gi_category is low per convention.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor sodium intake, as Fish Tikka can sometimes be high in salt from marinades and accompanying chutneys, which may contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index, high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fish Tikka is generally a good choice for managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high protein and low carbohydrate content, which helps in maintaining stable blood sugar levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean protein, healthy fats (omega-3 from fish), spices
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Made with lean fish and healthy spices, Fish Tikka can be a heart-healthy option, providing beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and protein that support cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Tikka is a popular Indian appetizer or main course featuring boneless pieces of fish, typically white fish like cod or basa. The fish is marinated in a mixture of yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and a blend of aromatic Indian spices such as turmeric, cumin, coriander, and red chili powder. After marination, the fish pieces are traditionally skewered and grilled in a tandoor (clay oven) or pan-fried until tender and slightly charred. It has a savory, mildly spicy, and tangy flavor profile with a tender, flaky texture. Nutritionally, Fish Tikka is high in protein and generally low in carbohydrates, making it a suitable option for those managing blood sugar or seeking lean protein sources. It is widely served in Indian restaurants across Singapore and is considered a halal-friendly, gluten-free dish.
common_user_queries: Is Fish Tikka good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient data for Fish Tikka is adapted from a generic grilled fish entry in USDA FoodData Central, with adjustments for typical Indian marinade ingredients. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on the low carbohydrate content of fish and yogurt. Multilingual names and ontology mappings are based on research and closest available matches.
